titleStmt.title: 201. Memorandum From the Acting Assistant Secretary of State for Inter-American Affairs (Rubottom) to the ) to the Acting Secretary of State

div.head: 201. Memorandum From the Acting Assistant Secretary of State for Inter-American Affairs (Rubottom) to the Acting Secretary of State Source: Department of State, Central Files, 713.00/12–1256. Secret.
div.p: Since I will be away from the Department for the rest of this week, I want to report to you, as of 5:45 p.m. today, the status of the following matters which have been of current interest and covered by me at your staff meetings for the past few days:

Nicaragua–Costa Rica. Guillermo Sevilla-Sacasa Nicaraguan Ambassador to the United States. replied to John Dreier’s telegram today expressing Nicaragua’s willingness to establish the joint boundary commission with Costa Rica called for under their agreement of January 1956 but heretofore not implemented by
either party. This is an encouraging development since the Costa Ricans are believed also ready to appoint their member to the commission, and should contribute to the relief of tensions between them.
[Here follow reports on Bolivia and Brazil.]
